summary: Security vulnerability testing completed for XSS protection, email validation, and security headers. Found critical issues with XSS protection implementation and space removal bug.

critical_bugs.issue: XSS Protection Not Working as Required - HTML tags are being HTML-encoded (< >) instead of being stripped completely
critical_bugs.details: When user enters '<script>alert(XSS)</script>', the sanitizeInput function stores '<script>alert(XSS)</script>' instead of stripping the tags. While HTML encoding prevents XSS execution, the requirement was to STRIP tags, not encode them. The sanitizeInput function works correctly when tested directly in console (returns empty string for script tags), but when used through React onChange handler, tags are being encoded.

critical_bugs.issue: Spaces Removed from Valid Input - Critical UX Bug
critical_bugs.details: When user enters valid names with spaces like 'John Doe' or 'Acme Corporation', the spaces are removed and stored as 'JohnDoe' or 'AcmeCorporation'. This breaks normal user input and is unacceptable for name fields.

minor_issues.issue: Error message shows only '*' instead of descriptive text when HTML tags detected
minor_issues.details: When XSS payload is entered, error message element shows only '*' character instead of the expected 'Special characters and HTML tags are not allowed' message

minor_issues.issue: Edge case email 'test..test@example.com' (double dots) not rejected
minor_issues.details: Email with consecutive dots before @ is technically invalid per RFC but not caught by validation

passed_tests: ✓ Security Headers: CSP (Content-Security-Policy) meta tag present in HTML
✓ Security Headers: X-Frame-Options meta tag present (set to DENY)
✓ Security Headers: X-Content-Type-Options meta tag present (set to nosniff)
✓ Email Validation: SQL injection pattern 'admin'--' correctly rejected
✓ Email Validation: SQL injection pattern 'test@example.com'; DROP TABLE users--' correctly rejected
✓ Email Validation: SQL injection pattern 'admin'OR'1'='1' correctly rejected
✓ Email Validation: SQL injection pattern 'test@test.com;DELETE FROM users' correctly rejected
✓ Email Validation: Invalid format 'notanemail' correctly rejected
✓ Email Validation: Invalid format '@example.com' correctly rejected
✓ Email Validation: Invalid format 'test@' correctly rejected
✓ Email Validation: Valid email 'john.doe@example.com' accepted without errors
✓ Email Validation: Valid email 'contact@acme.com' accepted without errors
✓ sanitizeInput function works correctly when tested directly in browser console
action_item_for_main_agent: CRITICAL: Fix two major bugs in sanitization: 1) HTML tags are being encoded instead of stripped - investigate why DOMPurify.sanitize() with ALLOWED_TAGS:[] returns HTML-encoded strings when used in React onChange handler (works correctly in console). 2) Spaces are being removed from valid input - this breaks all name fields. Root cause: DOMPurify.sanitize() is removing whitespace. Consider using a different approach or configuring DOMPurify to preserve spaces while stripping tags.

success_percentage.xss_protection: 0% (0/2 passed - tags encoded not stripped, spaces removed)
